content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`
Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun is the audit/debug run log for pipeline observability summary=Section 3.9 defines `MemoryRun` as an audit trail and debug layer to track prompt/model usage, retrieved items, budget, proposed vs applied operations, and pipeline failure points for each run. scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,pipeline observability tags=memory_model,doc_digest,memoryrun evidence=### 3.9 `MemoryRun` Audit trail и debug слой.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun recommended schema and invariants for idempotent, durable run logging summary=The section lists recommended `MemoryRun` fields (including `run_type`, `trigger_mode`, retrieval/prompt/model/IO/ops/status/error metrics) and states two invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to section 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun fields,run_type,trigger_mode,input_hash,retry safety tags=schema,invariants,memoryrun evidence=Инварианты: - run log не должен исчезать silently - `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 memory model summary=`Source` is the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check and explainability. It should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` separate from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit delete policy. scope=Applies to section 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_architecture,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Task entity usage rules and lifecycle invariants summary=In section 3.7, `Task` is operational memory for future actions and should be created only when real future action is required. Core invariants are: plain questions are not tasks, requests are not commitments, each task must represent one clear commitment, and status should be updated instead of creating duplicates; evidence linkage is handled via separate `task_sources` with `support_type` (`dire...[truncated 36 chars] scope=Applies to section 3.7 `Task` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Task,task_sources,task invariants,status update,commitment tags=data_model,task,invariants,evidence_linkage useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 data model uses layered memory architecture with two cross-cutting concerns summary=In `v3`, the data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle` are defined as cross-cutting concerns. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,layered memory architecture,scope,retrieval lifecycle tags=data_model,architecture,memory_layers,cross_cutting useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory layer with lifecycle and linkage summary=`Note` is explicitly positioned between raw sources and hard claims: a single self-contained semantic fragment for retrieval-worthy context (decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) with status/retrieval/maturity lifecycle, evidence linkage, entity linkage, and note-to-note relations. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,retrieval_state,maturity_state,note_entities,note_sources,note_links tags=data_model,memory_v3,note_design useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` defines reconciliation behavior per predicate summary=In the v3 memory model, `PredicateCatalog` is a configuration component (not a memory item) that controls how each predicate is reconciled, including cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and whether facts project into profile/task layers. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy,profile_sync,task_sync tags=data_model,memory_v3,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Episode is post-MVP optional memory for reusable lessons summary=Section 3.8 defines `Episode` as an optional memory type intended for later phases (not MVP) to preserve transferable experience such as successful tactics, failure patterns, and reusable lessons. Episodes must avoid raw chain-of-thought and store lesson-oriented outcomes rather than full reasoning traces. scope=Applies to section 3.8 `Episode` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Episode,MVP,lessons learned,failure patterns,chain-of-thought tags=data_model,episode,roadmap,policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Claim` schema role and quality invariants in v3 summary=In `02. Data Model` section 3.5, `Claim` is the atomic fact/relation unit for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval; each claim should represent one predicate application and remain self-contained, with explicit statements preferred over inference and weak/soft meaning redirected to `Note` instead of low-quality claims. scope=Applies to section 3.5 `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim,predicate-based reconciliation,claim_sources,invariants tags=data_model,v3,claim,schema_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge policy summary=`Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify mentions and anchor claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with alias and merge support. Merge must preserve history, aliases must resolve to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges must be reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to section 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Profile` as compact rebuildable projection layer summary=Section 3.6 defines `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection (not source of truth) built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in `Claim`/`Note`, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to section 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,v3,profile,invariants useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.89,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<2>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New section-specific digest for MemoryRun purpose (audit/debug observability) not yet captured in active notes.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.9,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<2>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Distinct reusable detail on MemoryRun schema fields and invariants (durability, input_hash idempotency) absent from existing notes.","target_note_id":null}]}
